title: Define minimal R1 HAL and devicetree boundary
description: Why this exists: the R1 MVP needs only enough HAL/Kconfig/devicetree structure to keep board-specific details out of protocol/app code. What needs to be done: finish the minimal pieces of project-LICHEN-h7t5.1 and project-LICHEN-h7t5.3 needed for radio, BLE local transport, identity, GNSS, battery/PMIC, buttons/LEDs, and board identity. Acceptance criteria: required capabilities are represented by explicit Kconfig/devicetree conventions; absent capabilities return deterministic unsupported/unavailable behavior; invalid combinations fail at build time where practical; three independent codereview subagents run after code changes and findings are recorded.
notes:
Implemented minimal Zephyr HAL/Kconfig/devicetree boundary for R1 MVP:
- Added CONFIG_LICHEN_HAL subsystem under lichen/subsys/lichen/hal with public <lichen/hal.h>.
- Added capability flags and board identity API for LoRa, BLE local, serial local, GNSS, battery, PMIC, buttons, LEDs, display, and external flash.
- Added radio model, UI profile, location provider, and uptime/GNSS time provider enums; deferred RTC provider until an RTC convention exists.
- Added deterministic device/status APIs: LoRa, GNSS, display, serial local, battery, PMIC, external flash, BLE local status, and GPIO dt-spec accessors for LED/button.
- Added build assertions for enabled capabilities: okay chosen zephyr,lora; okay GNSS/display/battery/PMIC/external flash aliases; okay serial chosen nodes; GPIO LED/button aliases with gpios; BLE HCI; radio-model compatibility with chosen zephyr,lora compatible.
- Added native_sim HAL ztest with default/headless absent-capability behavior and loopback-LoRa positive capability coverage.
- Added follow-up project-LICHEN-6mco for serial-local chosen precedence positive test coverage.

Subagent/review results recorded:
- Research agents covered HAL boundary, Zephyr Kconfig/DTS conventions, and R1/R1 Neo capability mapping.
- Initial codereview trio found radio model contradiction, chosen-node status gaps, missing positive/negative coverage, and missing device/status getters; fixed.
- Final codereview trios found LED/button device pointer contract bug, BLE status gap, SX127x/STM32WL default gaps, serial precedence mismatch, GPIO okay-status gap, and unvalidated RTC provider; fixed or deferred with follow-up project-LICHEN-6mco.

Verification on EC2 Zephyr cache:
- git diff --check: clean.
- west build -p always -b native_sim -d build/hal-native-default lichen/tests/hal && west build -d build/hal-native-default -t run: PASS.
- west build -p always -b native_sim -d build/hal-native-loopback lichen/tests/hal -- -DCONF_FILE="prj.conf;prj_lora_loopback.conf" -DDTC_OVERLAY_FILE=boards/native_sim_lora_loopback.overlay && west build -d build/hal-native-loopback -t run: PASS.
- west twister -T lichen/tests/hal -p native_sim --inline-logs --verbose: 2/2 scenarios PASS, 6 test cases executed, 0 warnings.
- Manual invalid radio model build (loopback DTS with LICHEN_RADIO_MODEL_SX126X) failed at expected static assertion.
